revision.text:
'''三本木'''（さんぼんぎ、さんぼぎ）
== 地名 == （さんぼんぎ） * [[三本木 (青森市)]] - [[青森県]][[青森市]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (十和田市)]] - 青森県[[十和田市]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (おいらせ町)]] - 青森県[[上北郡]][[おいらせ町]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (大崎市)]] - [[宮城県]][[大崎市]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (那須塩原市)]] - [[栃木県]][[那須塩原市]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (上三川町)]] - 栃木県[[河内郡]][[上三川町]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (五泉市)]] - [[新潟県]][[五泉市]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (大垣市)]] - [[岐阜県]][[大垣市]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (大治町)]] - [[愛知県]][[海部郡]][[大治町]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (京都市)]] - [[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]]の地名。
（さんぼぎ） * [[三本木 (藤岡市)]] - [[群馬県]][[藤岡市]]の地名。 <!-- == 姓 == * --> == 関連項目 == * [[三本木町]] * {{Prefix}} * [[一本木]] - [[二本木]] - [[四本木]] - [[五本木]] - [[六本木]] - [[七本木]]
{{地名の曖昧さ回避}} {{デフォルトソート:さんほんき}} <!--[[Category:日本語の姓]]-->

'''三本木'''（さんぼんぎ、さんぼぎ）
== 地名 == （さんぼんぎ） * [[三本木 (青森市)]] - [[青森県]][[青森市]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (十和田市)]] - 青森県[[十和田市]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (おいらせ町)]] - 青森県[[上北郡]][[おいらせ町]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (大崎市)]] - [[宮城県]][[大崎市]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (那須塩原市)]] - [[栃木県]][[那須塩原市]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (上三川町)]] - 栃木県[[河内郡]][[上三川町]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (五泉市)]] - [[新潟県]][[五泉市]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (大垣市)]] - [[岐阜県]][[大垣市]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (大治町)]] - [[愛知県]][[海部郡 (愛知県)|海部郡]][[大治町]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (京都市)]] - [[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]]の地名。
（さんぼぎ） * [[三本木 (藤岡市)]] - [[群馬県]][[藤岡市]]の地名。 <!-- == 姓 == * --> == 関連項目 == * [[三本木町]] * {{Prefix}} * [[一本木]] - [[二本木]] - [[四本木]] - [[五本木]] - [[六本木]] - [[七本木]]

revision.text: == Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors == {{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021}} The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10}}{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018}} --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|会津大塚山古墳}}|| Fukushima Prefecture, {{ill|Aizuwakamatsu|ja|会津若松市}} || || 4th century || Among the oldest in Tohoku, designated as a National Historic Site in 1972. |- ! colspan=5 | Kanto Region |- | {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Burial Mound|ja|前橋天神山古墳}} || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}} || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || Two Four-God Four-Beast Mirrors excavated. |- | {{ill|Tamonoko Burial Mound|ja|頼母子古墳}} (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}} || Unknown (Keyhole-shaped mound is likely) || Mid-4th century || Two triangular-rimmed mirrors (one location unknown). |- | {{ill|Kitayama Chausuyama Burial Mound|ja|北山茶臼山古墳}} || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Tomioka|ja|富岡市}} || Round mound (possible keyhole shape) || Late 4th century || |- | {{ill|Kanizawa Burial Mound|ja|蟹沢古墳}} (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}} || Round mound (estimated, possibly square mound) || || Mirror with "Zhengshi" (正始) inscription. Same mold as former Motoda No. 48 mound. |- | {{ill|Akagitsuka Burial Mound|ja|赤城塚古墳}} (Nishioka Shrine Burial Mound) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}} || Round mound || 4th century || Triangular-rimmed Buddha Beast Mirror excavated. |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|ja|三本木}} || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}} || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|ja|神田浅間神社古墳}}. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|ja|手古塚古墳}} (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}} || || || Same mold as Kawabe Burial Mound, Shimane Prefecture. |- | {{ill|Shiroyama No. 1 Burial Mound (Katori)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}} || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Komiyama Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-6th || Part of {{ill|Kawabe-Takamori Kofun Cluster|ja|川部・高森古墳群}}; Kyushu's oldest burial mound; five mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Mochida Burial Mounds|ja|持田古墳群}} || Miyazaki Prefecture, {{ill|Koyu Same mold as Maebashi Tenjinyama Mound<ref name="miyazaki" />. Extensively looted in the early Shōwa period<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1849|title=Cultural Properties of Miyazaki: Mochida Burial Mounds |accessdate=2018-10-23}}</ref>. |} [[category:Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror forks]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Important cultural properties of Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]]

== Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors == {{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021}} The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10}}{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound<ref name="miyazaki">{{Cite journal |和書 |author=Noriyuki Imashioya |year=2010 |title=The Formation and Evaluation of the Mochida Burial Mounds in Takanabe Town, Miyazaki Prefecture |journal=Miyazaki Cultural Studies Research Bulletin |issue=37 |publisher=Miyazaki Prefectural Library |pages=47-65 |crid=1522543655217461888 |url=https://www.lib.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/ct/other000001600/kiyouimashioya.pdf}}</ref><ref>{{Cite Kotobank |word=Mochida Burial Mounds |encyclopedia=Nipponica Encyclopedia |accessdate=2018-10-23}}</ref> |- | Mochida Old Mound No. 48 || Miyazaki Prefecture, Koyu District, Takanabe Town, Mochida || Round mound || || Part of Mochida Burial Mounds. Same mold as Maebashi Tenjinyama Mound<ref name="miyazaki" />. Extensively looted in the early Shōwa period<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1849|title=Cultural Properties of Miyazaki: Mochida Burial Mounds |accessdate=2018-10-23}}</ref>. |} [[category:Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror forks]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Important cultural properties of Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]]

header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021}} The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10}}{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018}} --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|en}}[[ja:三本木]] || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en}}[[ja:藤岡市]] || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:神田浅間神社古墳]]. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:手古塚古墳]] (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|en}}[[ja:木更津市]] || || || Same mold as Kawabe Burial Mound, Shimane Prefecture. |- | {{ill|Shiroyama No. 1 Burial Mound (Katori)|en}}[[ja:城山1号墳 (香取市)]] || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en}}[[ja:香取市]], Komiyama Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-6th century || Mirrors excavated from a later burial mound. |- | {{ill|Matoda Ōtsukayama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:真土大塚山古墳]] (destroyed) || Kanagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Hiratsuka, Kanagawa|en}}[[ja:平塚市]], Nishimatoda || || || |- | {{ill|Hakusan Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:白山古墳]] (destroyed) || Kanagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki City|en}}[[ja:川崎市]], Saiwai Ward || header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! mound; five mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Mochida Burial Mounds|en}}[[ja:持田古墳群]] || Miyazaki Prefecture, {{ill|Koyu District, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:児湯郡]], {{ill|Takanabe, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:高鍋町]], Mochida || || || Mirror inscribed "Heavenly King Sun and Moon." Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound<ref name="miyazaki">{{Cite journal |和書 |author=Noriyuki Imashioya |year=2010 |title=The Formation and Evaluation of the Mochida Burial Mounds in Takanabe Town, Miyazaki Prefecture |journal=Miyazaki Cultural Studies Research Bulletin |issue=37 |publisher=Miyazaki Prefectural Library |pages=47-65 |crid=1522543655217461888 |url=https://www.lib.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/ct/other000001600/kiyouimashioya.pdf]]</ref><ref>{{Cite Kotobank |word=Mochida Burial Mounds |encyclopedia=Nipponica Encyclopedia |accessdate=2018-10-23]]</ref> |- | Mochida Old Mound No. 48 || Miyazaki Prefecture, Koyu District, Takanabe Town, Mochida || Round mound || || Part of Mochida Burial Mounds. Same mold as Maebashi Tenjinyama Mound<ref name="miyazaki" />. header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|en}}[[ja:三本木]] || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en}}[[ja:藤岡市]] || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:神田浅間神社古墳]]. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:手古塚古墳]] (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|en}}[[ja:木更津市]] || || || Same mold as Kawabe Burial Mound, Shimane Prefecture. |- | {{ill|Shiroyama No. 1 Burial Mound (Katori)|en}}[[ja:城山1号墳 (香取市)]] || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en}}[[ja:香取市]], Komiyama Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-6th century || Mirrors excavated from a later burial mound. |- | {{ill|Matoda Ōtsukayama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:真土大塚山古墳]] (destroyed) || Kanagawa Prefecture, revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror forks]] to [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Extensively looted in the early Shōwa period<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1849|title=Cultural Properties of Miyazaki: Mochida Burial Mounds |accessdate=2018-10-23]]</ref>. |} [[Category:Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror forks]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Important cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Sanbongi]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Sanbongi]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Sanbongi]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Sanbongi]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sanbongi]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]]

revision.text:
header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|en}}[[ja:三本木]] || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en}}[[ja:藤岡市]] || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:神田浅間神社古墳]]. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:手古塚古墳]] (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|en}}[[ja:木更津市]] || || || Same mold as Kawabe Burial Mound, Shimane Prefecture. |- | {{ill|Shiroyama No. 1 Burial Mound (Katori)|en}}[[ja:城山1号墳 (香取市)]] || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en}}[[ja:香取市]], Komiyama Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-6th century || Mirrors excavated from a later burial mound. |- | {{ill|Matoda Ōtsukayama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:真土大塚山古墳]] (destroyed) || Kanagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Hiratsuka, Kanagawa|en}}[[ja:平塚市]], Nishimatoda || || || |- | {{ill|Hakusan Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:白山古墳]] (destroyed) || Kanagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki City|en}}[[ja:川崎市]], Saiwai Ward || Mound|en}}[[ja:小田中親王塚古墳]] || Ishikawa Prefecture, {{ill|Noto District, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:鹿島郡]] || Round mound || Late 4th century to end || |- | {{ill|Mori General's Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:森将軍塚古墳]] || revision.text:
header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:kofun]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! revision.text:
header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:kofun]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:kofun]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Extensively looted in the early Shōwa period<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1849|title=Cultural Properties of Miyazaki: Mochida Burial Mounds |accessdate=2018-10-23]]</ref>. |} [[Category:Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror forks]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sanbongi]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sanbongi]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sanbongi]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kofun]] [[Category:Important cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Sanbongi]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Sanbongi]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Sanbongi]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]][[Category:Sanbongi]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]]

header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:kofun]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:kofun]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:kofun]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:kofun]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:kk]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! '''Sanbongi''' (三本木, also pronounced Sanbogi)
== Place Names ==
(Sanbongi) * {{ill|Sanbongi, Aomori|ja|三本木 (青森市)}} – A district in {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Towada|ja|三本木 (十和田市)}} – A district in {{ill|Towada, Aomori|ja|十和田市}}, Aomori Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Oirase|ja|三本木 (おいらせ町)}} – A district in {{ill|Oirase, Kamikita District|ja|おいらせ町}}, {{ill|Kamikita District, Aomori|ja|上北郡}}, Aomori Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Ōsaki|ja|三本木 (大崎市)}} – A district in {{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Nasushiobara|ja|三本木 (那須塩原市)}} – A district in {{ill|Nasushiobara, Tochigi|ja|那須塩原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Kamikawachi District|ja|三本木 (上三川町)}} – A district in {{ill|Kaminokawa, Tochigi|ja|上三川町}}, {{ill|Kamikawachi District, Tochigi|ja|河内郡}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Gosen|ja|三本木 (五泉市)}} – A district in {{ill|Gosen, Niigata|ja|五泉市}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Ōgaki|ja|三本木 (大垣市)}} – A district in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Ōharu|ja|三本木 (大治町)}} – A district in {{ill|Ōharu, Aichi|ja|大治町}}, {{ill|Ama District, Aichi|ja|海部郡}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Kyoto|ja|三本木 (京都市)}} – A district in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}.
(Sanbogi) * {{ill|Sanbogi, Fujioka|ja|三本木 (藤岡市)}} – A district in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}.

'''Sanbongi''' (三本木, also pronounced Sanbogi)
== Place Names ==
(Sanbongi) * {{ill|Sanbongi, Aomori|ja|三本木 (青森市)}} – A district in {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Towada|ja|三本木 (十和田市)}} – A district in {{ill|Towada, Aomori|ja|十和田市}}, Aomori Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Oirase|ja|三本木 (おいらせ町)}} – A district in {{ill|Oirase, Kamikita District|ja|おいらせ町}}, {{ill|Kamikita District, Aomori|ja|上北郡}}, Aomori Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Ōsaki|ja|三本木 (大崎市)}} – A district in {{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Nasushiobara|ja|三本木 (那須塩原市)}} – A district in {{ill|Nasushiobara, Tochigi|ja|那須塩原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Kamikawachi District|ja|三本木 (上三川町)}} – A district in {{ill|Kaminokawa, Tochigi|ja|上三川町}}, {{ill|Kamikawachi District, Tochigi|ja|河内郡}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Gosen|ja|三本木 (五泉市)}} – A district in {{ill|Gosen, Niigata|ja|五泉市}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Ōgaki|ja|三本木 (大垣市)}} – A district in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Ōharu|ja|三本木 (大治町)}} – A district in {{ill|Ōharu, Aichi|ja|大治町}}, {{ill|Ama District, Aichi|ja|海部郡}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Kyoto|ja|三本木 (京都市)}} – A district in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}.
(Sanbogi) * {{ill|Sanbogi, Fujioka|ja|三本木 (藤岡市)}} – A district in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}.

'''Sanbongi''' (三本木, also pronounced Sanbogi)
== Place Names ==
(Sanbongi) * {{ill|Sanbongi, Aomori|ja|三本木 (青森市)}} – A district in {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Towada|ja|三本木 (十和田市)}} – A district in {{ill|Towada, Aomori|ja|十和田市}}, Aomori Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Oirase|ja|三本木 (おいらせ町)}} – A district in {{ill|Oirase, Kamikita District|ja|おいらせ町}}, {{ill|Kamikita District, Aomori|ja|上北郡}}, Aomori Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Miyagi|ja|三本木 (大崎市)|en}} – A district in {{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Nasushiobara|ja|三本木 (那須塩原市)}} – A district in {{ill|Nasushiobara, Tochigi|ja|那須塩原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Kamikawachi District|ja|三本木 (上三川町)}} – A district in {{ill|Kaminokawa, Tochigi|ja|上三川町}}, {{ill|Kamikawachi District, Tochigi|ja|河内郡}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Gosen|ja|三本木 (五泉市)}} – A district in {{ill|Gosen, Niigata|ja|五泉市}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Ōgaki|ja|三本木 (大垣市)}} – A district in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Ōharu|ja|三本木 (大治町)}} – A district in {{ill|Ōharu, Aichi|ja|大治町}}, {{ill|Ama District, Aichi|ja|海部郡}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Kyoto|ja|三本木 (京都市)}} – A district in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}.

'''Sanbongi''' (三本木, also pronounced Sanbogi)
== Place Names ==
(Sanbongi) * {{ill|Sanbongi, Aomori|ja|三本木 (青森市)}} – A district in {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Towada|ja|三本木 (十和田市)}} – A district in {{ill|Towada, Aomori|ja|十和田市}}, Aomori Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Oirase|ja|三本木 (おいらせ町)}} – A district in {{ill|Oirase, Kamikita District|ja|おいらせ町}}, {{ill|Kamikita District, Aomori|ja|上北郡}}, Aomori Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Miyagi|ja|三本木 (大崎市)|en}} – A district in {{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Nasushiobara|ja|三本木 (那須塩原市)}} – A district in {{ill|Nasushiobara, Tochigi|ja|那須塩原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Kamikawachi District|ja|三本木 (上三川町)}} – A district in {{ill|Kaminokawa, Tochigi|ja|上三川町}}, {{ill|Kamikawachi District, Tochigi|ja|河内郡}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Gosen|ja|三本木 (五泉市)}} – A district in {{ill|Gosen, Niigata|ja|五泉市}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Ōgaki|ja|三本木 (大垣市)}} – A district in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Ōharu|ja|三本木 (大治町)}} – A district in {{ill|Ōharu, Aichi|ja|大治町}}, {{ill|Ama District, Aichi|ja|海部郡}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Kyoto|ja|三本木 (京都市)}} – A district in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}.
(Sanbogi) * {{ill|Sanbogi, Fujioka|ja|三本木 (藤岡市)}} – A district in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}.

'''Sanbongi''' (三本木, also pronounced Sanbogi)
== Place Names ==
(Sanbongi) * {{ill|Sanbongi, Aomori|ja|三本木 (青森市)}} – A district in {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Towada|ja|三本木 (十和田市)}} – A district in {{ill|Towada, Aomori|ja|十和田市}}, Aomori Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Oirase|ja|三本木 (おいらせ町)}} – A district in {{ill|Oirase, Kamikita District|ja|おいらせ町}}, {{ill|Kamikita District, Aomori|ja|上北郡}}, Aomori Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Miyagi|ja|三本木 (大崎市)|en}} – A district in {{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Nasushiobara|ja|三本木 (那須塩原市)}} – A district in {{ill|Nasushiobara, Tochigi|ja|那須塩原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Kamikawachi District|ja|三本木 (上三川町)}} – A district in {{ill|Kaminokawa, Tochigi|ja|上三川町}}, {{ill|Kamikawachi District, Tochigi|ja|河内郡}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Gosen|ja|三本木 (五泉市)}} – A district in {{ill|Gosen, Niigata|ja|五泉市}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Ōgaki|ja|三本木 (大垣市)}} – A district in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Ōharu|ja|三本木 (大治町)}} – A district in {{ill|Ōharu, Aichi|ja|大治町}}, {{ill|Ama District, Aichi|ja|海部郡}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Kyoto|ja|三本木 (京都市)}} – A district in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}.
(Sanbogi) * {{ill|Sanbogi, Fujioka|ja|三本木 (藤岡市)}} – A district in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}.

'''Sanbongi''' (三本木, also pronounced Sanbogi)
== Place Names ==
(Sanbongi) * {{ill|Sanbongi, Aomori|ja|三本木 (青森市)}} – A district in {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Towada|ja|三本木 (十和田市)}} – A district in {{ill|Towada, Aomori|ja|十和田市}}, Aomori Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Oirase|ja|三本木 (おいらせ町)}} – A district in {{ill|Oirase, Kamikita District|ja|おいらせ町}}, {{ill|Kamikita District, Aomori|ja|上北郡}}, Aomori Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Miyagi|ja|三本木 (大崎市)|en}} – A district in {{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Nasushiobara|ja|三本木 (那須塩原市)}} – A district in {{ill|Nasushiobara, Tochigi|ja|那須塩原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Kamikawachi District|ja|三本木 (上三川町)}} – A district in {{ill|Kaminokawa, Tochigi|ja|上三川町}}, {{ill|Kamikawachi District, Tochigi|ja|河内郡}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Gosen|ja|三本木 (五泉市)}} – A district in {{ill|Gosen, Niigata|ja|五泉市}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Ōgaki|ja|三本木 (大垣市)}} – A district in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Ōharu|ja|三本木 (大治町)}} – A district in {{ill|Ōharu, Aichi|ja|大治町}}, {{ill|Ama District, Aichi|ja|海部郡}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Kyoto|ja|三本木 (京都市)}} – A district in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}.

(Sanbongi) * {{ill|Sanbongi, Aomori|ja|三本木 (青森市)}} – A district in {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Towada|ja|三本木 (十和田市)}} – A district in {{ill|Towada, Aomori|ja|十和田市}}, Aomori Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Oirase|ja|三本木 (おいらせ町)}} – A district in {{ill|Oirase, Kamikita District|ja|おいらせ町}}, {{ill|Kamikita District, Aomori|ja|上北郡}}, Aomori Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Miyagi|ja|三本木 (大崎市)|en}} – A district in {{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Nasushiobara|ja|三本木 (那須塩原市)}} – A district in {{ill|Nasushiobara, Tochigi|ja|那須塩原市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Kamikawachi District|ja|三本木 (上三川町)}} – A district in {{ill|Kaminokawa, Tochigi|ja|上三川町}}, {{ill|Kamikawachi District, Tochigi|ja|河内郡}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Gosen|ja|三本木 (五泉市)}} – A district in {{ill|Gosen, Niigata|ja|五泉市}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Ōgaki|ja|三本木 (大垣市)}} – A district in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Ōharu|ja|三本木 (大治町)}} – A district in {{ill|Ōharu, Aichi|ja|大治町}}, {{ill|Ama District, Aichi|ja|海部郡}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Kyoto|ja|三本木 (京都市)}} – A district in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|上京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}.
(Sanbogi) * {{ill|Sanbogi, Fujioka|ja|三本木 (藤岡市)}} – A district in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Sanbongi Town|ja|三本木町}} * {{ill|Prefix|ja|Prefix}} * {{ill|Ippongi|ja|一本木}} – {{ill|Nihongi|ja|二本木}} – {{ill|Shihongi|ja|四本木}} – {{ill|Gohongi|ja|五本木}} – {{ill|Roppongi|ja|六本木}} – {{ill|Shichihongi|ja|七本木}}

'''Sanbongi''' (三本木, also pronounced Sanbogi)
== Place Names ==
(Sanbongi) * {{ill|Sanbongi, Aomori|ja|三本木 (青森市)|12=draft}} – A district in {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Towada|ja|三本木 (十和田市)|12=draft}} – A district in {{ill|Towada, Aomori|ja|十和田市|12=draft}}, Aomori Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Oirase|ja|三本木 (おいらせ町)|12=draft}} – A district in {{ill|Oirase, Kamikita District|ja|おいらせ町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamikita District, Aomori|ja|上北郡|12=draft}}, Aomori Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Miyagi|ja|三本木 (大崎市)|en|12=draft}} – A district in {{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Nasushiobara|ja|三本木 (那須塩原市)|12=draft}} – A district in {{ill|Nasushiobara, Tochigi|ja|那須塩原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Kamikawachi District|ja|三本木 (上三川町)|12=draft}} – A district in {{ill|Kaminokawa, Tochigi|ja|上三川町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamikawachi District, Tochigi|ja|河内郡|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Gosen|ja|三本木 (五泉市)|12=draft}} – A district in {{ill|Gosen, Niigata|ja|五泉市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Ōgaki|ja|三本木 (大垣市)|12=draft}} – A district in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Ōharu|ja|三本木 (大治町)|12=draft}} – A district in {{ill|Ōharu, Aichi|ja|大治町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ama District, Aichi|ja|海部郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Sanbongi, Kyoto|ja|三本木 (京都市)|12=draft}} – A district in {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|上京区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}.
(Sanbogi) * {{ill|Sanbogi, Fujioka|ja|三本木 (藤岡市)|12=draft}} – A district in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Sanbongi Town|ja|三本木町|12=draft}} * {{ill|Prefix|ja|Prefix|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ippongi|ja|一本木|12=draft}} – {{ill|Nihongi|ja|二本木|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shihongi|ja|四本木|12=draft}} – {{ill|Gohongi|ja|五本木|12=draft}} – {{ill|Roppongi|ja|六本木|12=draft}} – {{ill|Shichihongi|ja|七本木|12=draft}}

'''三本木'''（さんぼんぎ、さんぼぎ）
== 地名 == （さんぼんぎ） * [[三本木 (青森市)]] - [[青森県]][[青森市]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (十和田市)]] - 青森県[[十和田市]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (おいらせ町)]] - 青森県[[上北郡]][[おいらせ町]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (大崎市)]] - [[宮城県]][[大崎市]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (那須塩原市)]] - [[栃木県]][[那須塩原市]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (上三川町)]] - 栃木県[[河内郡]][[上三川町]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (五泉市)]] - [[新潟県]][[五泉市]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (大垣市)]] - [[岐阜県]][[大垣市]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (大治町)]] - [[愛知県]][[海部郡 (愛知県)|海部郡]][[大治町]]の地名。 * [[三本木 (京都市)]] - [[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]]の地名。
（さんぼぎ） * [[三本木 (藤岡市)]] - [[群馬県]][[藤岡市]]の地名。 <!-- == 姓 == * --> == 関連項目 == * [[三本木町]] * {{Prefix}} * [[一本木]] - [[二本木]] - [[四本木]] - [[五本木]] - [[六本木]] - [[七本木]]
{{地名の曖昧さ回避}} {{デフォルトソート:さんほんき}} <!--[[Category:日本語の姓]]-->
